devanagari: सगरचरित्रम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । हरितो रोहितसुतः चंपः तस्माद्विनिर्मिता । चंपापुरी सुदेवोऽतो विजयो यस्य चात्मजः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: sagaracaritram śrīśuka uvāca harito rohitasutaḥ caṃpaḥ tasmādvinirmitā caṃpāpurī sudevo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Modern Nandgaon, close to Bharata Kuṇḍa, eight or nine miles South of Fyzabad (U.P.)—N.L.De—GDAMI, p. 138.

devanagari: भरुकस्तत्सुतस्तस्माद् वृकस्तस्यापि बाहुकः । सोऽरिभिर्हृतभू राजा सभार्यो वनमाविशत् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: bharukastatsutastasmād vṛkastasyāpi bāhukaḥ so
translation: He was originally a Gandharva Prince Viśvāvasu who was blessed with immortality by god Brahmā. He arrogantly attacked Indra who, with his Vajra squeezed his head and thighs into his body. When he begged for livelihood, he was given two long arms and a mouth on the belly. He was asked to stay near Mātaṅgāśrama in Krauñcāraṇya—a part of Daṇḍakāraṇya. When Rāma and Lakṣmaṇa came after Jaṭāyu’s funeral, he caught hold of them. Rāma and Lakṣmaṇa cut his hands. He asked who they were and then told them his life story and fell dead. When they cremated his body, a Gandharva King (Kabandha’s former self) rose in the air, advised Rāma to go to Sugrīva for more information about Sītā.—Vālmiki Rāmāyaṇa Araṇya Kāṇḍa—Sargas 70-72.

devanagari: वृद्धं तं पञ्चतां प्राप्तं महिष्यनु मरिष्यती । और्वेण जानतात्मानं प्रजावन्तं निवारिता ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: vṛddhaṃ taṃ pañcatāṃ prāptaṃ mahiṣyanu mariṣyatī aurveṇa jānatātmānaṃ prajāvantaṃ nivāritā

translation: His life and works have been described in details and also heard by sages who have clearly understood the Truth. The story of the Lord of Sītā might have been listened by you many times.

devanagari: आज्ञायास्यै सपत्‍नीभिः गरो दत्तोऽन्धसा सह । सह तेनैव सञ्जातः सगराख्यो महायशाः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: ājñāyāsyai sapat‍nībhiḥ garo datto saha tenaiva sañjātaḥ sagarākhyo mahāyaśāḥ

devanagari: सगरश्चक्रवर्त्यासीत् सागरो यत्सुतैः कृतः । यस्तालजंघान् यवनात् शकान् हैहयबर्बरान् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: sagaraścakravartyāsīt sāgaro yatsutaiḥ kṛtaḥ yastālajaṃghān yavanāt śakān haihayabarbarān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]
[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ han ], [vocative single from √ han ]

translation: May that Lord of the Kosala (country) protect us—the Lord who gave up his kingdom for honouring the pledge given by his father, and wandered from forest to forest on his lotus-feet that were too tender to bear the touch of the palms of his beloved Sītā (who pressed and massaged them to relieve their fatigue), and whose fatigue from journey was soothed by the leader of the monkeys (Hanumān or Sugrīva) and his younger brother Lakṣmaṇa; the Lord who constructed a bridge at the request of the deity presiding over the ocean, which was terrified at the display of his frowning eyebrows, excited by his wrath at the separation from his beloved, caused (by Rāvaṇa who was incited to do so) by disfiguring of Śūrpaṇakhā (by Lakṣmaṇa’s mutilating her nose and ears), and thus who was like a conflagration to the forest (in the form) of the wicked (demons).

devanagari: नावधीद् गुरुवाक्येन चक्रे विकृतवेषिणः । मुण्डान् श्मश्रुधरान् कांश्चित् मुक्तकेशार्धमुण्डितान् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: nāvadhīd guruvākyena cakre vikṛtaveṣiṇaḥ muṇḍān śmaśrudharān kāṃścit muktakeśārdhamuṇḍitān

translation: During the sacrificial session of Viśvāmitra, prominent demons and nocturnal predators like Mārīca and others were (single-handedly) despatched by him while Lakṣmaṇa was simply witnessing.

devanagari: अनन्तर्वाससः कांश्चिद् अबहिर्वाससोऽपरान् । सोऽश्वमेधैरयजत सर्ववेदसुरात्मकम् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: anantarvāsasaḥ kāṃścid abahirvāsaso so

[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: In the hall specially erected for Sītā’s free election of her (prospective) husband, wherein were assembled eminent warriors of the world, Rāma, like unto a cub of an elephant sportively picking up a sugar-cane, lifted up the formidable bow of god Śiva which required three hundred bearers to bring it (at the hall), strung it, drew it and broke it in the middle, oh King.

devanagari: और्वोपदिष्टयोगेन हरिमात्मानमीश्वरम् । तस्योत्सृष्टं पशुं यज्ञे जहाराश्वं पुरन्दरः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: aurvopadiṣṭayogena harimātmānamīśvaram tasyotsṛṣṭaṃ paśuṃ yajñe jahārāśvaṃ purandaraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Having won (as a prize) Śrī, the beautiful goddess of prosperity, who had already a place of honour on his bosom,—but is now called Sītā, who was eminently suitable to him in beauty, virtues, disposition, age and form, Rāma, on his way (to Ayodhyā), humbled down the overbearing pride and arrogance of Paraśurāma, the chief of Bhṛgus who extirpated the Kṣatriya race from the earth thrice seven times.

devanagari: सुमत्यास्तनया दृप्ताः पितुरादेशकारिणः । हयं अन्वेषमाणास्ते समन्तात् न्यखनन् मन्महीम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: sumatyāstanayā dṛptāḥ piturādeśakāriṇaḥ hayaṃ anveṣamāṇāste samantāt nyakhanan manmahīm

translation: He honoured with his head (bent low) the order of his henpecked father who was bound by the noose of truthfulness (pledge to grant two boons to Kaikeyī who now demanded kingdom for her son and banishment of Rāma to forest). With his spouse, he relinquished his kingdom, wealth, his beloved relations (like his mother etc.), friends and palace and left for the forest just as a yogin who has renounced all attachments, would give up his body.

devanagari: प्राग् उदीच्यां दिशि हयं ददृशुः कपिलान्तिके । एष वाजिहरश्चौर आस्ते मीलितलोचनः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: prāg udīcyāṃ diśi hayaṃ dadṛśuḥ kapilāntike eṣa vājiharaścaura āste mīlitalocanaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[vocative single from √ mīl class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mīl ]
[vocative single from √ mīl class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mīl ]

translation: While (armed) with an irresistible bow in his hand, he went about leading a hard life (in the forest as a model for mankind to follow), he got mutilated the features of the wicked-minded sister of Rāvaṇa (viz. Śūrpaṇakhā who wanted to devour Sītā with a view to enjoy amorous life with Rāma etc.), and killed fourteen thousand of her kinsmen of whom Khara, Triśiras and Dūṣaṇa were prominent.

devanagari: हन्यतां हन्यतां पाप इति षष्टिसहस्रिणः । उदायुधा अभिययुः उन्मिमेष तदा मुनिः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: hanyatāṃ hanyatāṃ pāpa iti ṣaṣṭisahasriṇaḥ udāyudhā abhiyayuḥ unmimeṣa tadā muniḥ
translation: When, during his absence (from the hermitage) in the forest, the princess of Videha (Sītā) was abducted by the vilest demon Rāvaṇa like a wolf (stealthily carrying away a sheep), Rāma, being separated from his beloved, wandered in the forest with his brother Lakṣmaṇa simulating pitiable helplessness{GL_NOTE::} demonstrating (to the world) the tragic fate of those who are attached to women.

devanagari: स्वशरीराग्निना तावन् महेन्द्रहृतचेतसः । महद्व्यतिक्रमहता भस्मसाद् अभवन् क्षणात् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: svaśarīrāgninā tāvan mahendrahṛtacetasaḥ mahadvyatikramahatā bhasmasād abhavan kṣaṇāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ han class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ han class 2 verb]

devanagari: न साधुवादो मुनिकोपभर्जिता । नृपेन्द्रपुत्रा इति सत्त्वधामनि । कथं तमो रोषमयं विभाव्यते । जगत्पवित्रात्मनि खे रजो भुवः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: na sādhuvādo munikopabharjitā nṛpendraputrā iti sattvadhāmani kathaṃ tamo roṣamayaṃ vibhāvyate jagatpavitrātmani khe rajo bhuvaḥ
translation: (When, even after a fast of consecutive three nights, the presiding deity of the sea did not present himself, Rāma flared up in anger). The sea (its presiding deity) with alligators and acquatic animals in whom got panicky and bewildered, at the cast of his side-glance in apparent anger, and with its roar hushed up in terror, assumed a (human) form and taking on his head articles of worship approached his (Rāma’s) lotus-like feet and spoke as follows:

devanagari: यस्येरिता सांख्यमयी दृढेह नौः । यया मुमुक्षुस्तरते दुरत्ययम् । भवार्णवं मृत्युपथं विपश्चितः । परात्मभूतस्य कथं पृथङ्मतिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: yasyeritā sāṃkhyamayī dṛḍheha nauḥ yayā mumukṣustarate duratyayam bhavārṇavaṃ mṛtyupathaṃ vipaścitaḥ parātmabhūtasya kathaṃ pṛthaṅmatiḥ
translation: “We, dull-witted fellows, did not know you definitely to be the Immutable, Eternal (First) Person, the overlord of the Universe, Oh Supreme Lord. You are the Lord of Prakṛti (the source of guṇas), from the Sattva-guṇa out of which are evolved the celestials, from the rajas, the prajāpatis (Lord of creation) and from tamas, the goblin-leaders and other evil spirits.

devanagari: योऽसमञ्जस इत्युक्तः स केशिन्या नृपात्मजः । तस्य पुत्रोऽंशुमान्नाम पितामहहिते रतः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: yo tasya putro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vac class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ vac class 3 verb]
[nominative dual from √ hi class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ hi class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ hi class 5 verb], [locative single from √ hi class 5 verb]
[nominative dual from √ hi class 5 verb], [vocative single from √ hi class 5 verb], [vocative dual from √ hi class 5 verb], [accusative dual from √ hi class 5 verb]

translation: May you be pleased to cross me at your sweet will. Do kill Ṛāvaṇa, the excreation of Viśravas who makes the three world shriek (in fear). Regain your consort, Oh hero! (Although my waters will not obstruct your movements) do construct a bridge here for the spread of your glory which (future) kings, conquerors of the quarters (world) reaching here, will sing.”

devanagari: असमञ्जस आत्मानं दर्शयन् असमञ्जसम् । जातिस्मरः पुरा संगाद् योगी योगाद् विचालितः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: asamañjasa ātmānaṃ darśayan asamañjasam jātismaraḥ purā saṃgād yogī yogād vicālitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ dṛś ], [vocative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: Having constructed a bridge across the sea with mountain peaks of various sizes, covered with trees the branches of which were shaken off by monkey chiefs, Rāma, the foremost in the Raghu’s race, under the supervision and guidance of Vibhīṣaṇa, and accompanied by the forces of Vānara chiefs like Sugrīva, Nīla, Hanumān, entered Laṅkā which was already burnt by (Hanumān) previously.

devanagari: आचरन् गर्हितं लोके ज्ञातीनां कर्म विप्रियम् । सरय्वां क्रीडतो बालान् प्रास्यदुद्वेजयञ्जनम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: ācaran garhitaṃ loke jñātīnāṃ karma vipriyam sarayvāṃ krīḍato bālān prāsyadudvejayañjanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ krīḍ class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vij ], [vocative single from √ vij ]

translation: Like a river churned up by herds of elephants, the city of Laṅkā was in a state of commotion when its playgrounds, barns and granaries, treasuries, doorways, citygates, assembly-halls, roofs, balconies and pigeon-cots were barricaded and besieged by the armies of the monkey-chiefs, and platforms, flagstaffs, gold-vases (on either sides of entrances) and public squares were destroyed.

devanagari: एवंवृत्तः परित्यक्तः पित्रा स्नेहमपोह्य वै । योगैश्वर्येण बालान् तान् दर्शयित्वा ततो ययौ ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃvṛttaḥ parityaktaḥ pitrā snehamapohya vai yogaiśvaryeṇa bālān tān darśayitvā tato yayau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Observing the state of siege, the king of Rākṣasas (Rāvaṇa) despatched against them all his followers like Nikumbha, Kumbha, Dhūmrākṣa, Durmukha, Surāntaka, Narāntaka and others, and his sons Indrajit, Prahasta, Atikāya, Vikampana and others, and finally (his brother) Kumbhakarṇa.

devanagari: अयोध्यावासिनः सर्वे बालकान् पुनरागतान् । दृष्ट्वा विसिस्मिरे राजन्राजा चाप्यन्वतप्यत ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: ayodhyāvāsinaḥ sarve bālakān punarāgatān dṛṣṭvā visismire rājanrājā cāpyanvatapyata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]

translation: Accompanied by Sugrīva, Lakṣmaṇa, Hanumān, Gandhamāda, Nīla, Aṅgada, Jāmbavān the bear, Panasa and others, Rāma marched against the Rākṣasa army impenetrable on account of its weaponry like swords, spikes, bows, prāsas (darts), double-edged swords, lances, arrows, iron clubs and long swords.

devanagari: अंशुमांश्चोदितो राज्ञा तुरगान्वेषणे ययौ । पितृव्यखातानुपथं भस्मान्ति ददृशे हयम् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: aṃśumāṃścodito rājñā turagānveṣaṇe yayau pitṛvyakhātānupathaṃ bhasmānti dadṛśe hayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Aṅgada and all the generals of Rāma’s forces fell upon the Rākṣasa army consisting of battalions of elephants, infantry, chariots and cavalry, and engaged them in duels and struck them down with trees, rocks, maces and arrows, as their Lord Rāvaṇa had lost his auspicious luck by the touch of Sītā (in abduction).

devanagari: तत्रासीनं मुनिं वीक्ष्य कपिलाख्यं अधोक्षजम् । अस्तौत् समाहितमनाः प्राञ्जलिः प्रणतो महान् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tatrāsīnaṃ muniṃ vīkṣya kapilākhyaṃ adhokṣajam astaut samāhitamanāḥ prāñjaliḥ praṇato mahān
translation: Perceiving the destruction of his armies, Rāvaṇa, the king of Rākṣasas, got enraged. Ascending his aerial car Puṣpaka, he attacked Rāma who rode resplendent in a refulgent chariot brought from the celestial region by Mātali (the charioteer of god Indra). Rāvaṇa struck Rāma with sharp arrows with horse-shoe-shaped heads.

devanagari: अंशुमानुवाच । न पश्यति त्वां परमात्मनोऽजनो । न बुध्यतेऽद्यापि समाधियुक्तिभिः । कुतोऽपरे तस्य मनःशरीरधी । विसर्गसृष्टा वयमप्रकाशाः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: aṃśumānuvāca na paśyati tvāṃ paramātmano na budhyate kuto visargasṛṣṭā vayamaprakāśāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle third single], [present passive third single]
[nominative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ sṛj class 6 verb]

translation: Rāma scolded him: “You, excretion of a man- eater! During our absence our beloved was taken away by you, a wicked fellow, even as a dog (steals away things when the house-owner is away). To you, a shameless fellow, I, like the god of death of irresistible power meting out the punishment to the perpetrator of a sin for his act, shall award the fruit of your detestable action, just now.”

devanagari: ये देहभाजस्त्रिगुणप्रधाना । गुणान् विपश्यन्त्युत वा तमश्च । यन्मायया मोहितचेतसस्ते । विदुः स्वसंस्थं न बहिःप्रकाशाः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: ye dehabhājastriguṇapradhānā guṇān vipaśyantyuta vā tamaśca yanmāyayā mohitacetasaste viduḥ svasaṃsthaṃ na bahiḥprakāśāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ śā class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ śā class 4 verb], [vocative dual from √ śā class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śā class 4 verb], [accusative dual from √ śā class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śā class 4 verb]

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Reproaching Ṛāvaṇa thus, Rāma shot his arrow that was already fixed on the bow. It pierced Rāvaṇa’s heart as if with the impact of the thunderbolt. Like a meritorious person whose balance of good acts is exhausted, Rāvaṇa fell from his aerial car, with blood gushing out of his ten mouths, while his partisans cried out ‘Alas!’.

devanagari: तं त्वां अहं ज्ञानघनं स्वभाव । प्रध्वस्तमायागुणभेदमोहैः । सनन्दनाद्यैर्मुनिभिर्विभाव्यं । कथं विमूढः परिभावयामि ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tvāṃ ahaṃ jñānaghanaṃ svabhāva pradhvastamāyāguṇabhedamohaiḥ sanandanādyairmunibhirvibhāvyaṃ kathaṃ vimūḍhaḥ paribhāvayāmi
translation: Thousands of Rākṣasa women (whose husbands were killed{GL_NOTE::}) along with Mandodarī (Rāvaṇa’s chief queen) came out of Laṅkā and rushed to the battle-field1 wailing loudly.

devanagari: प्रशान्त मायागुणकर्मलिंगं । अनामरूपं सदसद्विमुक्तम् । ज्ञानोपदेशाय गृहीतदेहं । नमामहे त्वां पुरुषं पुराणम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: praśānta māyāguṇakarmaliṃgaṃ anāmarūpaṃ sadasadvimuktam jñānopadeśāya gṛhītadehaṃ namāmahe tvāṃ puruṣaṃ purāṇam
translation: They embraced their respective relatives who were struck down by Lakṣmaṇa’s arrows. Beating themselves (their breasts with their own hands), those helpless ladies lamented loudly.

devanagari: त्वन्मायारचिते लोके वस्तुबुद्ध्या गृहादिषु । भ्रमन्ति कामलोभेर्ष्या मोहविभ्रान्तचेतसः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: tvanmāyāracite loke vastubuddhyā gṛhādiṣu bhramanti kāmalobherṣyā mohavibhrāntacetasaḥ
translation: “Alas! Oh Rāvaṇa! Oh Lord! The terror of the world! Undone we are! Bereft of you, now trampled over by the enemies, to whom can Laṅkā look up for protection?

devanagari: अद्य नः सर्वभूतात्मन् कामकर्मेन्द्रियाशयः । मोहपाशो दृढश्छिन्नो भगवन् तव दर्शनात् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: adya naḥ sarvabhūtātman kāmakarmendriyāśayaḥ mohapāśo dṛḍhaśchinno bhagavan tava darśanāt
translation: Oh highly blessed glorious Lord! Being overwhelmed with the passion of love, you did not estimate and anticipate the potential superforce of Sītā’s virtue whereby you have been reduced to this wretched condition.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्थं गीतानुभावस्तं भगवान् कपिलो मुनिः । अंशुमन्तं उवाचेदं अनुग्राह्य धिया नृप ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvāca itthaṃ gītānubhāvastaṃ bhagavān kapilo muniḥ aṃśumantaṃ uvācedaṃ anugrāhya dhiyā nṛpa

translation: Widowed is Laṅkā and we as well, Oh delight of your family! Your body has become now food for vultures, your soul fit for hell.”

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । अश्वोऽयं नीयतां वत्स पितामहपशुस्तव । इमे च पितरो दग्धा गंगाम्भोऽर्हन्ति नेतरत् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvāca aśvo ime ca pitaro dagdhā gaṃgāmbho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Under instructions from Rāma, the king of Kośalas Vibhīṣaṇa performed the prescribed funeral rites known as ‘sacrifice to manes’, of his relatives.

devanagari: तं परिक्रम्य शिरसा प्रसाद्य हयमानयत् । सगरस्तेन पशुना यज्ञशेषं समापयत् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ parikramya śirasā prasādya hayamānayat sagarastena paśunā yajñaśeṣaṃ samāpayat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Thereafter, the glorious Lord Rāma caught sight of Sītā sitting under a Śiṃśipā tree, in the retreat of the Aśoka forest. She was emaciated and suffering from the (mental) agony, due to her separation from him.

devanagari: राज्यं अंशुमते न्यस्य निःस्पृहो मुक्तबन्धनः । और्वोपदिष्टमार्गेण लेभे गतिमनुत्तमाम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: rājyaṃ aṃśumate nyasya niḥspṛho muktabandhanaḥ aurvopadiṣṭamārgeṇa lebhe gatimanuttamām

[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation:
Perceiving his most beloved wife in that helpless condition, Rāma was moved with compassion, even though her lotus-like face began to blossom with delight, at the sight of his own self.
32-34(A). Having conferred on Vibhīṣaṇa the sovereignty over Laṅkā, and Lordship over the Rākṣasas, and the life-span of a Kalpa (a period of the life span of fourteen Manus), the glorious Lord placed her in the aerial car Puṣ- paka and accompanied by brothers (Lakṣmaṇa and adopted brother Sugrīva) and Hanumān, Rāma ascended Puṣpaka. As he had completed the vow (of forest-dwelling for fourteen years), he returned to his capital (Ayodhyā), being showered on the way with flowers offered by the presiding deities of various regions, and his glorious deeds being joyously sung by gods like Brahmā and others.
34(B)-36. When Rāma heard that his brother put on bark-garments only, and subsisted on barley seeds boiled in cow’s urine, had grown matted hair and slept on bare ground with Kuśa grass spread over it, being extremely merciful by nature, he was deeply distressed.
